items.title: JPMorgan Marks Down Software Loans as Private Credit Risks Emerge

items.title: Treasury Prices Drop After Back-to-Back Weak Auctions
items.description: The war in the Middle East, and its impact on a volatile stock market, should have created conditions for investors to run to Treasuries, a longtime haven.  Investors were awarded 4.217% during the 10-year note sale, which was 0.6 basis points above the yield seen before the bidding deadline.  Higher rates at auctions signal that demand is weak, as the Treasury has to entice investors to buy U.S. debt.
